---
title: "Lecture 04 Notes: Robot Modeling I - Rigid Body Motions"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 04 slides](../slides/02-mathematical-foundations-se3.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Robot geometry is described using coordinate frames and rigid transforms.
- Homogeneous transforms unify rotation and translation into one matrix form.
- Composition order matters because matrix multiplication is not commutative.

## Core representation

\[
T =
\begin{bmatrix}
R & p \\
0 & 1
\end{bmatrix}, \quad R \in SO(3), \; T \in SE(3)
\]

## Review questions

1. Why do we use homogeneous coordinates in robotics?
2. What is the geometric meaning of \(T_{ab} T_{bc}\)?
3. How do you compute \(T^{-1}\) efficiently from \(R\) and \(p\)?
